tier1_twin_cities.description: Highest-value Twin Cities sources to subscribe to first. Each has free bid notification email subscriptions.

sources.name: Minnesota Office of State Procurement
sources.notes: Register in the Supplier Portal; select commodity codes for paint, flooring, multi-family maintenance.

sources.name: Minneapolis Public Housing Authority (MPHA)
sources.notes: eProcurement registration; PRIMARY unit-turn source — register as paint, flooring, general maintenance vendor.

sources.name: Saint Paul Public Housing Agency (SPPHA)
sources.notes: PRIMARY unit-turn source. Section 3 contractor program available.

sources.name: Hennepin County Procurement
sources.notes: ProcureWare platform; can subscribe to specific commodity categories.

sources.name: Ramsey County Procurement
sources.notes: Email notification list available.

sources.name: City of Minneapolis
sources.notes: Subscribe via City eSupplier portal.

sources.name: City of Saint Paul
sources.notes: Vendor registration with HREEO.

sources.name: Minneapolis Public Schools
sources.notes: Summer maintenance season is peak.

sources.name: Saint Paul Public Schools (SPPS)
sources.notes: Summer maintenance season is peak.

tier2_statewide_expansion.description: Add after Tier 1 is validated. Targets the unit-turn-heavy verticals where material-clearance arbitrage compounds.

sources.name: All Minnesota Public Housing Authorities (~150 county/city PHAs)
sources.notes: Each PHA runs its own procurement. Goldmine for unit turns — every PHA has constant turn work.

sources.name: Minnesota school districts (~330 districts)
sources.notes: Summer paint/flooring season. Many use SourceWell or BidNet.

sources.name: Aeon (nonprofit affordable housing)
sources.notes: Owns/manages 5,300+ apartments in MN. Major unit turn volume.

sources.name: CommonBond Communities
sources.notes: 13,000+ affordable units across MN/WI/IA.

sources.name: Project for Pride in Living (PPL)
sources.notes: Mpls-based, affordable housing + workforce training.

sources.name: Dominium Apartments
sources.notes: Major MN-based multifamily owner/operator.

sources.name: Ecumen (senior living)
sources.notes: 50+ senior communities across MN.

sources.name: Minnesota State (MnSCU)
sources.notes: Dorm turns, classroom paint, athletic facility refresh.

sources.name: Mille Lacs Corporate Ventures (Grand Casino, etc.)
sources.notes: Hotel and casino paint, flooring, room renovation work.

sources.name: SAM.gov (federal)
sources.notes: VA hospitals (Mpls VA), Fort Snelling federal buildings. Requires SAM registration.

sources.name: Insurance carrier vendor programs (State Farm, USAA, Allstate)
sources.notes: Periodically open new-vendor calls. Track press releases / industry newsletters.

tier3_productized.description: Once the tool is reliable for Fishbeck internally, package as a SaaS for non-competing contractors (HVAC, electrical, plumbing, roofing) or out-of-state markets (WI/IA/Dakotas).
tier3_productized.notes: Multi-tenant architecture: every record carries tenant_id from day one so this is a flip-the-switch upgrade, not a rewrite.
